intro: Building a gaming PC doesn't have to be overwhelming. Our free calculator helps you estimate your total budget based on your target frame rate—whether you're aiming for smooth 60 FPS gaming at 1080p or ultra-competitive 240 FPS. Get GPU and CPU recommendations instantly, with prices updated for 2026.
keyTakeaway: **Formula: CPU + GPU + peripherals**. Enter your target FPS and game year, get instant budget recommendations and component suggestions based on 2026 pricing.

explanation:
## Building the Right Gaming PC

Performance matters when choosing gaming PC components. Whether you prioritize ultra-high frame rates for competitive esports or cinematic quality at 4K resolution, our calculator helps you find the sweet spot between cost and performance.

## How It Works

This calculator uses your target frame rate and resolution to recommend the right GPU and CPU pairing. It estimates total system cost including main components, based on 2026 market pricing.

## Budget Breakdown

- **1080p 60 FPS**: $600–$800 (RTX 3060, RX 7600)
- **1080p 144 FPS**: $1,000–$1,300 (RTX 4060 Ti, RX 7700 XT)
- **1440p 144 FPS**: $1,500–$2,000 (RTX 4070)
- **4K 60 FPS**: $1,500–$2,000 (RTX 4070 Super)
- **Competitive 240 FPS**: $1,800–$2,500 (RTX 4070 Ti, RX 7900 XTX)

## Important Notes

This calculator provides estimates based on 2026 component pricing. Actual costs vary by retailer, availability, and region. Always comparison shop before purchasing. Add $200–$300 for peripherals and monitor.

faq.q: How much should I budget for a 1080p 60 FPS gaming PC?
faq.a: $600–$800. GPU options: RTX 3060 or RX 7600. Good for 1080p high-setting gaming.

faq.q: What's the budget for 144 FPS at 1080p?
faq.a: $1,000–$1,300. RTX 4060 Ti or RX 7700 XT recommended. Excellent for competitive games.

faq.q: How much for a 1440p 144 FPS gaming PC?
faq.a: $1,500–$2,000. RTX 4070 or similar delivers balanced 1440p performance.

faq.q: What's the cost for 4K 60 FPS gaming?
faq.a: $1,500–$2,000. RTX 4070 Super recommended. Plays AAA titles at high settings in 4K.

faq.q: How much does a competitive 240 FPS gaming rig cost?
faq.a: $1,800–$2,500. RTX 4070 Ti or RX 7900 XTX. Ultra-low latency for esports.

faq.q: Should I budget for a monitor and peripherals?
faq.a: Yes, add $200–$300 for keyboard, mouse, headset, and monitor. A 144+ Hz monitor is essential.
Yes. All Hacé Cuentas calculators are completely free with no registration required.

faq.q: What's the difference between 60 FPS and 144 FPS gaming?
faq.a: 144 FPS is smoother and more responsive, essential for competitive shooters. 60 FPS is sufficient for casual gaming.

sources.name: NIST — National Institute of Standards and Technology

dataUpdate.notes: Fixed formula, no external data updates needed
